message:
fix: eliminate unnecessary allocation in truncate_width

The truncation branch was returning Cow::Owned(s[..idx].to_string())
which allocates a new String, but s[..idx] is already a slice of the
input string. Changed to Cow::Borrowed(&s[..idx]) for zero-copy in
all code paths. Also simplified the no-truncation path to return
Cow::Borrowed(s) directly instead of tracking end_idx.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
